findings.detail: Two-input converge (Precision + Recall --> F1) reads cleanly left-to-right; the lopsided-case contrast panel sits side-by-side on the right. Plain average shown red (0.5 misleading), Harmonic mean/F1 shown green (0 correct) — color now carries the misleading-vs-correct semantics. Title banner carries the governing takeaway. No overlaps, no truncation, subtitles legible at viewer scale.

cycle_1_fixed: whitespace_imbalance / side_panels_hollow: contrast panel had a large empty orange interior — tightened nodeSpacing/rankSpacing so cells size naturally.
color_tier_violation: avg/harm were yellow(hitl)/purple(auto) with no meaning — re-tiered to red 'bad' for the misleading plain average and green 'good' for the correct harmonic result.
edge label: 'uses' understated the relationship — changed to 'is the' and relabeled the node 'Harmonic mean (F1)' to make the F1==harmonic-mean point explicit.
